questions.text: What is the primary demand of Allah in the beginning of Surah Az-Zumar?
questions.answer: Sincere, pure devotion/religion (Ad-Deen al-Khalis) to Him alone.

questions.text: What is the excuse polytheists give for worshipping others besides Allah (Verse 3)?
questions.answer: They say 'We only worship them so that they may bring us nearer to Allah in station'.

questions.text: In how many 'darknesses' are humans created in the wombs of their mothers?
questions.answer: Three darknesses.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'skin' of those who fear their Lord when they hear the Quran?
questions.answer: Their skins shiver, then their skins and hearts relax at the remembrance of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Best Hadith' (Ahsan al-Hadith) mentioned in Verse 23?
questions.answer: The Book (the Quran), consistent and repeating.

questions.text: What is the parable of the man with multiple 'quarrelling partners' (Verse 29)?
questions.answer: It compares a polytheist (serving many conflicting masters) with a monotheist (serving one master); they are not equal.

questions.text: What is the famous message of hope for those who have 'transgressed against themselves' (Verse 53)?
questions.answer: 'Do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful'.

questions.text: What will happen to the Earth on the Day of Judgment (Verse 67)?
questions.answer: The entire earth will be within His grip, and the heavens will be folded in His right hand.

questions.text: How many times will the Trumpet be blown in this Surah's description?
questions.answer: Two times: the first causes all to fall dead/unconscious, and the second causes them to stand up looking on.

questions.text: How will the disbelievers be driven to Hell?
questions.answer: In groups (Zumara).

questions.text: What will the keepers of Hell ask the disbelievers upon their arrival?
questions.answer: 'Did there not come to you messengers from among yourselves, reciting the verses of your Lord?'.

questions.text: How will the righteous be led to Paradise?
questions.answer: In groups (Zumara), and its gates will be opened for them.

questions.text: What is the greeting of the keepers of Paradise to the believers?
questions.answer: 'Peace be upon you; you have become pure; so enter it to abide eternally therein'.

questions.text: What will the believers say when they enter Paradise?
questions.answer: 'Praise to Allah, who has fulfilled for us His promise and made us inherit the earth [of Paradise]'.

questions.text: What is the 'Az-Zumar' that the Surah is named after?
questions.answer: The groups in which people will be led to their final destinations (Heaven or Hell).

questions.text: What is the 'loss' mentioned in Verse 15?
questions.answer: The loss of one's self and one's family on the Day of Resurrection; that is the manifest loss.

questions.text: To whom belongs 'Intercession' (Shafa'ah) entirely?
questions.answer: To Allah (Verse 44).

questions.text: What happens to the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter when Allah is mentioned alone?
questions.answer: Their hearts shrink with aversion.

questions.text: What does Allah do with the souls during sleep (Verse 42)?
questions.answer: He takes them; He keeps those for which death has been decreed and releases the others for a specified term.

questions.text: What is the status of the 'Truthful' (those who bring the truth and believe it)?
questions.answer: They are the 'Muttaqun' (the righteous/God-fearing).
